item.title: Release MOCPilot 2026.1.0
item.content:encoded:
🚀 MOCPilot 2026.1.0 — Feature & Stability Update​

We’re happy to announce the release of MOCPilot 2026.1.0, now available on Android, iOS, and macOS.

This release introduces guest mode, improves usability on mobile devices, and delivers important stability and performance fixes across all supported platforms.

✨ What’s New in 2026.1.0?​

🆕 New​

  • Guest mode — use the app without an account, with an option to create one later
  • Language selection added to the authentication screen
  • The app now blocks usage of hubs with unsupported firmware and shows a clear warning

⚙️ Improvements​

  • Improved block dragging behavior on mobile devices
  • Blocks palette now hides automatically while dragging on mobile
  • Alert dialogs now apply a blur effect to the background
  • Improved text input handling across all platforms
  • Smoother hub control with reduced latency

🛠 Fixes​

  • Fixed multiple block drag & drop issues, including stuck blocks and lost mouse/touch input
  • Fixed crashes when stopping programs that use custom (“My”) blocks
  • Fixed steering rack calibration not returning to center position
  • Fixed hub control lag and delayed motor response
  • Fixed issues with editor ports, sliders, and speed value blocks
  • Fixed main dialog and various UI layout issues
  • Updated translations and fixed minor visual inconsistencies

Update MOCPilot to the latest version and enjoy a smoother, more stable, and more accessible experience across all your devices.

item.title: Releases MOCPilot 2025.2.0 - 2025.2.4
item.content:encoded:
🚀 MOCPilot 2025.2.0 - 2025.2.4 — Stability & Experience Update​

We’re happy to announce the MOCPilot 2025.2.0 - 2025.2.4 release, now available on Android, iOS, and macOS.

This release focuses on smoother behavior, improved reliability, and UI refinements across all supported platforms, delivering a more stable and polished experience for everyday use.

✨ What’s Improved in 2025.2.0 - 2025.2.4?​

  • Smoother and more stable behavior thanks to improved timing logic
  • Improved profile synchronization and update reliability
  • Refined profile list layout with clearer information display
  • Enhanced aura slider visuals and interaction feedback
  • Fixed pitch and roll inversion issues in control blocks
  • Improved UI layouts on phone and tablet devices
  • Fixed color, switcher, and visual consistency issues
  • Improved translations and text consistency
  • General bug fixes, performance improvements, and increased stability

Update MOCPilot to the latest version and enjoy a more refined, reliable, and responsive experience across all your devices.

item.title: Release MOCPilot 2025.2.0
item.content:encoded:
🚀 Introducing MOCPilot 2025.2.0 — Official Multi-Platform Release!​

We’re excited to announce the official release of MOCPilot 2025.2.0, now available across Android, iOS, and macOS!

This update marks a major step forward for the project. The application previously known as Controlz has been renamed to MOCPilot.
The new name better reflects the app’s purpose and future direction — serving as a unified “pilot” for your LEGO®-based creations, providing advanced control, programming capabilities, and a more intuitive experience for builders of all levels.

✨ What’s New in 2025.2.0?​

  • Unified multi-platform release for Android, iOS, and macOS
  • Improved stability, performance, and UI responsiveness
  • Updated branding and identity under the new name MOCPilot
  • Continued support for LEGO® Control+, BuWizz 2/3, and custom Bluetooth modules

Download MOCPilot today on your preferred platform and take full control of your LEGO® creations with the next-generation building and programming experience!

item.title: Release MOCPilot 2025.1.9
item.description: 🚀 MOCPilot 2025.1.9 is now available on the Mac App Store!
item.content:encoded:
🚀 MOCPilot 2025.1.9 is now available on the Mac App Store!​

We’re excited to announce the release of MOCPilot 2025.1.9 as a native macOS application!

You can now enjoy full desktop performance and seamless integration with macOS. Download it today from the Mac App Store and take control of your LEGO® creations like never before!

item.title: Release MOCPilot 2025.1.5
item.content:encoded:
Description​

This update enhances the block editing experience, improves performance, and ensures proper functionality on newer Android versions.

Changes​

  • Fixed a bug with hub scanning on Android 12+ by requesting precise location permission.
  • Added option to hide/show the blocks palette by clicking on the currently selected block collection.
  • Added a resize handle to the blocks palette to allow manual resizing.
  • Fixed high CPU usage when a profile was loaded but the program wasn’t running.
  • Improved the "String list" block editor to avoid content being cut off. Now items are split into columns for better visibility with large lists.

item.title: Release MOCPilot 2025.1.4
item.content:encoded:
Description​

This update improves support for tablets, adds useful testing pages, and fixes several compatibility and performance issues.

Changes​

  • Added tablet layout for better utilization of larger screens.
  • Fixed a bug where the "hub button pressed" block appeared for hubs that do not support this action. Also fixed inappropriate color sensor blocks for unsupported hubs (e.g., BuWizz).
  • Fixed a bug where the "motor run" block didn’t affect motors without encoders due to missing alternate run commands.
  • Added a page for testing gamepad buttons.
  • Added a hub testing page with a dashboard showing information, connected port devices, and port shake status.
  • Fixed incorrect accelerometer data interpretation for BuWizz 2 and 3, which caused faulty orientation readings.
  • Fixed RAM memory leaks that led to app termination on some devices due to high system memory usage.
  • Fixed bugs in profile loading that previously caused crashes in certain scenarios.
  • Fixed an Android bug where releasing gamepad sticks did not reset axis values to zero.

item.title: Release MOCPilot 2025.1.2 - 2025.1.3
item.content:encoded:
Description​

This update introduces new joystick customization options, UI improvements, and important stability fixes.

Changes​

  • Fixed a bug in the blocks palette collection selector: selecting a collection, scrolling to another, and returning had no effect.
  • Fixed a crash when loading heavy profiles with a large number of blocks.
  • Added “Axes type” property to joystick settings to support single-axis mode (X, Y, XY).
  • Added colorization for steering wheel controllers (“GMZ”, “No Mercy”).
  • Added “is pressed” blocks for steering wheels and pedals.

item.title: Release MOCPilot 2025.1.1
item.content:encoded:
Description​

This update includes several important bug fixes that enhance performance, reduce battery consumption, and improve block behavior in the programming environment.

Changes​

  • Fixed a bug causing high CPU usage, battery drain, and low performance.
  • Fixed a bug where gamepad blocks (e.g., “is button pressed”, “is D-PAD button pressed”) couldn’t be attached to other blocks.
  • Fixed an issue in the step switch event block that incorrectly handled touch events and step counting.
  • Fixed a bug in the list block’s “item of” where no more than one block could be attached as an argument index.

item.title: MOCPilot 2025.1.0
item.content:encoded:
🚀 MOCPilot 2025.1.0 Closed Beta Has Begun!​

We're excited to announce that the closed beta test for MOCPilot 2025 is now live! Selected users can now explore new features, provide feedback, and help shape the future of MOCPilot.

If you've received an invitation, check your email for access details. Thanks for being part of this important phase!
